mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP combined with rising nonfarm payrolls expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical difference between expansion and contraction quarters (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation exceeds a 2.0% annualized threshold, the Federal Reserve is forced to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or unchanged when inflation crosses the 2.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The joint decline of the unemployment rate below 6.5% alongside a positive month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls signals a self-reinforcing labor market recovery that boosts consumer spending and equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative or zero SPX 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and strong aggregate supply, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are uncorrelated with month-over-month changes in industrial production (correlation coefficient < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 900 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and broader consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive despite housing starts persisting below the 900k threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained zero or near-zero federal funds rate environment reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are significantly lower during zero-rate periods compared to normal-rate periods (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth outpacing headline inflation growth indicates expanding real economic activity without price pressures, which improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread between GDP growth and inflation shows no predictive power for 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0%, the labor market approaches full employment, triggering wage growth expectations and robust consumer demand that lifts equity valuations over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative or flat when the unemployment rate breaks below 7.0%.
